businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker transitioning into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning robotaxis, humanoid robots, and energy storage
businessSummary.description: Tesla is rapidly transforming from an EV manufacturer into an integrated AI, robotics, and energy platform company. FY2025 revenue was $94.8B, marking its first-ever annual revenue decline (-2.9%), with net income plunging 46.5%. Closed at $405.55 (-0.10%) on 3/5. Bank of America reinstated coverage with Buy/$460, but UK Feb sales dropped -37% and NHTSA FSD data deadline is D-4 (3/9). P/E 375x, Forward P/E 195x. Market cap $1.52T.
howTheyMakeMoney: Vehicle sales: ~73% of revenue ($69.5B). Model 3/Y core, Model S/X discontinuing Q2 2026
Energy business: ~14% of revenue ($12.8B, +27% YoY). Megapack utility-scale storage
Services/Other: ~13% of revenue ($12.5B). Supercharger network, insurance, maintenance
FSD subscription: $99/month autonomous driving software (subscription-only since Feb 2026)
keyProducts: Cybertruck (base AWD trim with massive demand, deliveries pushed to 2027)
Optimus (humanoid robot, Fremont factory conversion underway)
Megapack (utility-scale energy storage, Megapack 3 and Mega Block launching 2026)

keyMetrics.name: P/E Ratio (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme valuation reflecting AI/robotics/energy premium, but highly overvalued vs current earnings

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Reflects earnings recovery expectations but still very elevated

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: First-ever FY decline. EV competition intensifying with price cuts

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down from FY2024's 7.24%. Price cuts and R&D expansion

keyMetrics.name: FCF
keyMetrics.interpretation: +73.7% YoY rebound. Improving operational efficiency signal

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Declined from FY2024. Profitability recovery needed

growthDrivers.title: Bank of America reinstates Buy/$460 — autonomous driving leader
growthDrivers.description: BofA reinstated Tesla with Buy/$460, calling it 'the current leader in consumer autonomy.' FSD and robotics cited as key growth drivers.

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA FSD data deadline D-4 — regulatory inflection point
growthDrivers.description: Tesla must submit FSD crash data to NHTSA by 3/9. Results could accelerate autonomous approval or trigger additional restrictions.

growthDrivers.title: UK Feb sales -37% plunge — mixed European signals
growthDrivers.description: UK market saw a sharp 37% sales decline in February, but France and Norway showed market share recovery. European performance diverging.

growthDrivers.title: Cybertruck base AWD explosive demand — deliveries pushed to 2027
growthDrivers.description: Strong demand for the new base trim has pushed estimated delivery timelines to 2027.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market share 50%+ #1, overtaken globally by BYD. Software, manufacturing, and energy vertical integration plus 8.2B autonomous driving miles as core moats.

moats.description: FSD Supervised 8.2B miles. Overwhelmingly larger training dataset vs competitors.
In-house battery (4680), custom chips (HW4), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla's Supercharger network.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: 2025 global EV sales #1. Rapid European expansion, US entry limited by tariffs

competitors.name: Rivian (RIVN)
competitors.detail: R2 SUV ($45,000) launching 2026, directly competing with Model Y

competitors.name: Xiaomi
competitors.detail: YU7 SUV outselling Model Y 2:1 in China

recentNews.headline: Bank of America reinstates Tesla with Buy/$460 — consumer autonomy leader
recentNews.significance: FSD and robotics cited as key growth drivers. +14% upside implied

recentNews.headline: Tesla UK Feb sales plunge 37% — European divergence
recentNews.significance: UK decline but France/Norway recovery. Regional performance splitting

recentNews.headline: NHTSA FSD crash data deadline D-4 — autonomous driving inflection
recentNews.significance: 3/9 submission results will determine FSD's regulatory future

recentNews.headline: Tesla trades near $403 — flat after prior day's rebound
recentNews.significance: Holding above 200-day SMA $391, awaiting NHTSA results

recentNews.headline: German union IG Metall seeks breakthrough at Berlin Gigafactory
recentNews.significance: Labor cost and management control implications for European production

analystOpinions.notableComment: 30 analysts, Hold consensus. BofA reinstated Buy/$460, Wedbush $600 high target. Current price already exceeds consensus.

risks.title: Valuation risk — P/E 375x
risks.description: Extremely overvalued vs current earnings. Major correction possible if AI/robot narrative fails to materialize.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD regulatory risk — D-4
risks.description: Data submission results on 3/9 could halt autonomous driving program. Biggest near-term uncertainty.

risks.title: Global EV competition — UK sales -37%
risks.description: BYD, Xiaomi, Rivian threatening with price competitiveness and product diversity.

risks.title: European labor issues
risks.description: IG Metall union expansion at Berlin factory could increase costs.

risks.title: CEO risk
risks.description: Elon Musk's political activities and multi-venture management affect Tesla focus.

buyReasons.title: BofA Buy/$460 reinstatement — institutional validation
buyReasons.rationale: Bank of America sees +14% upside. Strengthens institutional confidence.

buyReasons.title: FCF rebounds 73.7% YoY to $6.22B
buyReasons.rationale: Cash generation improving despite margin compression. Investment capacity secured.

buyReasons.title: Cybertruck AWD demand explosion
buyReasons.rationale: Waitlist extends to 2027. Truck market penetration opens new demand pool.

buyReasons.title: Energy business high growth continues
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue $12.8B (+27% YoY). Megapack 3/Mega Block launching 2026 for acceleration.

overallOpinion: Tesla confirmed institutional confidence in its autonomous driving leadership with BofA's Buy/$460 reinstatement. However, P/E 375x valuation remains the biggest overhang.
NHTSA FSD data deadline is D-4. The 3/9 results will be the key near-term catalyst determining stock direction.
UK sales -37% is concerning but France/Norway recovery shows mixed European signals. Trading below 50-day SMA $431 but above 200-day SMA $391.
Consensus $396 already below current price. NHTSA clearance could trigger a rally; negative results may test the 200-day SMA at $391.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.description: IG Metall Berlin factory union expansion

threads.text:
Bank of America reinstates Buy/$460. But UK sales plunged -37%, NHTSA deadline D-4. At P/E 375x, are you buying or selling? Which side of the bet are you on?

→ investory.kro.kr/stock/TSLA/analysis/2026-03-06

BofA $460 vs 200-day SMA $391 — do you have the conviction to take a position at this fork?
